oFFlcE oF: crry CLERK (7r4) 736-220r
815 WEST STXTH STREET (P.O. BOX 940), CORONA, CALIFORNIA 9',1718-0090
May 3, l99l
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804
Re: Request for Technical Assistance
Gentlemen:
Councilmember Bill Franklin asked me to request advice from you concerning his employ-
ment status.
Mr. Franklin is employed as a loan officer for a Riverside-based mortgage company. He
works with other loan officers and real estate brokers who process retail sales of residential
property in a large portion of Western Riverside County, including Corona.
Mr. Franklin believes he is required to abstain from voting on any matter being considered
by the City Council due to a conflict if his employer is involved in that matter, whether or
not he is a party to the transaction. He has asked his manager for information that would
assist him in making this determination, but has been advised that the information can not
be given to him due to the'right to privacy' of the individuals involved and because he is
not the loan officer involved in the transaction. He receives a commission on his loan sales
only and does not receive any remuneration from other company transactions.
Mr. Franklin does not wish to sever his employment with this firm or change professions to
enabte him to remain on the City Council. He is extremely concerned with this situation
because of the conflict of interest laws. He feels he is in a 'Catch 22' and needs advice
from the Commission as to how he must respond under this set of circumstances when a
land matter is considered by the City Council.
Your prompt reply will be greatly appreciated by Mr. Franklin. Please respond to him di-
rectly at the above address.
City Clerk
Bill Franklin
Dallas Holmes of Best, Best & Krieger
City Attorney

sections.question: Mr. Franklin is employed as a loan officer for a Riverside-based mortgage company and is a Councilmember. He believes he is required to abstain from voting on any matter being considered by the City Council due to a conflict if his employer is involved in that transaction, whether or not he is a party to the transaction. He receives a commission on his loan sales only and does not receive remuneration from other company transactions. He has asked his manager for information to assist in making a conflict determination, but has been denied access due to privacy concerns and because he is not the loan officer involved in the transaction. How must he respond under conflict of interest laws when a land matter is considered by the City Council?
sections.question_synthetic: A city councilmember employed as a loan officer at a mortgage company seeks guidance on whether he must abstain from voting on city matters involving his employer's transactions, and how to obtain necessary information when access has been denied due to privacy claims.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No question or conclusion sections are explicitly labeled, but the substance of the inquiry is clear from the narrative. The letter requests prompt FPPC reply. Document formatted as formal correspondence from City Clerk to FPPC.

He has asked his manager for information to assist in making a conflict determination, but has been denied access due to privacy concerns and because he is not the loan officer involved in the transaction. embedding.summary: An incoming request from the City of Corona asking the FPPC for technical assistance regarding Councilmember Bill Franklin's employment conflict of interest. Franklin works as a loan officer for a mortgage company and needs advice on whether he must abstain from voting on matters involving his employer, and how to obtain necessary transaction information when his manager denies access citing privacy concerns.
